text: Show autonomy runtime ownership directly in the watchdog panel
description: Project Settings now shows runtime ownership and autonomy policy, but the watchdog panel should also make it obvious which policy/runtime state currently governs session restore, lease behavior, and animator ownership.

reflections.context: Show autonomy runtime ownership directly in the watchdog panel so users can see which policy governs session behavior without opening Project Settings.
reflections.outcome: Added a runtime info bar between the panel header and terminals that displays: owner label (e.g. 'Claude (Autonomous)', 'Supervised', 'Manual'), animator state (Enabled/Disabled/Emergency Stopped), and session lease duration. The bar appears when connected and both autonomyPolicy and runtimeMetadata are available.
reflections.effectiveness: Reusing the existing hydrateRuntimeState function was efficient - just added storage of runtimeMetadata to make it accessible in the component. The info bar design is minimal and non-intrusive, showing key information at a glance.
reflections.lesson: When adding UI features, check if the backend data is already being fetched - often just storing it in the right place is all that's needed. The watchdog store already hydrated runtime state; I just needed to expose it for display.
reflections.pattern: This follows a common pattern of progressive disclosure: summary information visible immediately (runtime info bar), detailed configuration available in settings modal. Users can see the active policy at a glance without opening a separate screen.

status.comment: Added runtime ownership info bar to watchdog panel showing autonomy mode, animator state, and session lease.
